projectName: Kafue Gorge Lower Hydropower Station — Equity Contribution
description: IDC equity contribution to the 750 MW Kafue Gorge Lower hydropower project, Zambia's largest power generation investment. The project will significantly increase national generation capacity and reduce load shedding.
expectedImpact: 750 MW additional generation capacity; reduction in load shedding from 12 hours to under 4 hours per day; 1,200 construction jobs and 350 permanent operational jobs.
risks: Commissioning delays due to civil works completion; foreign exchange risk on USD-denominated equipment costs.

projectName: Zambia Fertiliser and Chemicals Company (ZAFCO) Acquisition
description: IDC acquisition of a controlling stake in Zambia Fertiliser and Chemicals Company to support the Government's agricultural input supply strategy and reduce dependence on imported fertilisers.
expectedImpact: Domestic fertiliser production capacity of 120,000 MT per annum; reduced import bill; support for 500,000 smallholder farmers through subsidised input supply.
risks: Due diligence findings on environmental liabilities; regulatory approvals from Competition and Consumer Protection Commission.

description: Development of a 150-hectare industrial park on the outskirts of Lusaka to attract manufacturing investment, create jobs, and support import substitution. IDC is the anchor developer and will lease serviced plots to investors.
expectedImpact: 150 hectares of serviced industrial land; 50 manufacturing enterprises; 8,000 direct jobs; ZMW 1.2 billion in annual manufacturing output.
risks: Infrastructure connection costs higher than budgeted; investor uptake slower than projected due to macroeconomic conditions.

projectName: Zambia Gold Company — Ndola Gold Refinery Expansion
description: Expansion of the Ndola Gold Refinery to increase processing capacity from 2 MT to 5 MT of gold per annum, enabling Zambia to refine all domestically produced gold and capture value-added export revenues.
expectedImpact: 5 MT annual gold refining capacity; ZMW 420 million additional annual revenue; 180 new jobs at the refinery.
risks: Equipment procurement delays; skilled labour availability for refinery operations.

description: Rehabilitation of 180 km of mainline track on the Livingstone-Mazabuka corridor, the most critical section of the Zambia Railways network for copper export freight. Funded by GRZ capital injection and IDC bridging facility.
expectedImpact: Restoration of 80 km/h operating speed on rehabilitated section; 40% increase in freight capacity; reduction in derailment incidents.
risks: Contractor performance risk; weather delays during rainy season; procurement of track materials subject to import lead times.
